Idiomatic Expressions

  • 每早晚出馌 - Compound phrase meaning 'every morning and evening she went out to deliver food to her husband or family working in the fields.'
  • 辄见女子随左右 - 'Whenever she went, she would see a woman following her on both sides.' *辄* (zhé) is a classical particle meaning 'whenever' or 'always.'
  • 意大恐怖 - 'She felt a great fear.' *意* (yì) here means 'thought' or 'mind,' and *大恐怖* (dà kǒngbù) is 'great fear.'
  • 知为冤对 - 'She realized it was a vengeful spirit.' *冤对* (yuān duì) refers to a wronged spirit seeking revenge.
  • 因遥问之 - 'She then asked it from a distance.' *遥问* (yáo wèn) means 'to ask from afar.'
  • 以奸盗诬我 - 'You framed me with theft and adultery.' *诬* (wū) means 'to falsely accuse.'
  • 致幽死 - 'Causing me to be buried alive.' *幽死* (yōu sǐ) refers to a secret or unjust death, often used in the context of being wrongfully killed and buried.
  • 作道场度我 - 'Hold a Buddhist ritual to save me.' *道场* (dàochǎng) is a Buddhist term for a ritual or ceremony, and *度* (dù) means 'to save' or 'to liberate.'
  • 转轮 - 'To be reborn' or 'to be reborn in the cycle of samsara.' *转轮* (zhuǎn lún) is a Buddhist term related to the cycle of rebirth.
  • 解冤矣 - 'To resolve the grudge.' *解冤* (jiě yuān) means 'to resolve a grudge.'
  • 发念诵佛号万声 - 'To mentally recite the Buddha’s name ten thousand times.' *发念* (fā niàn) means 'to generate the thought or intention,' and *佛号* (fó hào) is the name of the Buddha.
  • 诚感神明 - 'Truly moved the divine.' *诚* (chéng) means 'sincerely' or 'truly,' and *感神明* (gǎn shénmíng) is 'to move the gods or divine beings.'
  • 声闻三界 - 'The sound reaches the three realms.' *三界* (sān jiè) refers to the three realms of existence in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 笃志事亲 - 'Dedicated in filial piety.' *笃志* (dǔ zhì) means 'steadfast in purpose' or 'devoted,' and *事亲* (shì qīn) is 'to serve one’s parents.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冤对 - A term from Buddhist and Taoist traditions referring to vengeful spirits or karmic retribution.
  • 道场 - A Buddhist ritual or ceremony, often held to liberate spirits or perform merit-making.
  • 佛号 - The name of the Buddha, such as Amitabha Buddha, which is commonly recited in Buddhist practice.
  • 转轮 - A Buddhist term referring to the cycle of rebirth (*samsara*), and also the concept of the Wheel of Dharma.
  • 三界 - The three realms of existence in Buddhist cosmology: desire, form, and formlessness.
  • 孝妇 - A woman known for her filial piety, a highly valued virtue in Confucian ethics.
  • 笃志事亲 - A phrase emphasizing the moral ideal of filial devotion, central to Confucian teachings.
  • 老时说之 - 'It is said by the elders,' indicating that the story is a traditional or folk tale passed down through generations.

Proper Nouns

  • 佛 - 'Buddha,' a proper noun referring to the historical figure Siddhartha Gautama.

  • 心有此事之象 - Literally 'the heart has the image of this matter,' referring to the mental or spiritual imprint of actions.
  • 冥司断狱 - Refers to the judgment of the underworld or afterlife, a common theme in Chinese supernatural literature.
  • 有心无心别善恶 - Literally 'distinguishing good and evil by whether there is intention or not,' a key moral principle in the text.
  • 神镜何以能照心 - Literally 'how can the divine mirror reflect the heart,' introducing a discussion of the mechanics of moral judgment.
  • 心不可见，缘物以形 - Literally 'the heart is not visible, but it is manifested through things,' a philosophical statement about the nature of the soul.
  • 体魂已离，存者性灵 - Literally 'the body and soul have separated, but what remains is the spiritual essence,' referring to the afterlife or spiritual existence.

  • 如灯荧荧，外光无翳，内光虚明 - A metaphor comparing the spiritual essence to a lamp, with outer light unobstructed and inner light clear, symbolizing clarity and transparency.
  • 内外莹澈，故纤芥必呈也 - Literally 'the inner and outer are clear and bright, so even the smallest things must be revealed,' emphasizing the thoroughness of divine judgment.
  • 遽曳之行 - Literally 'immediately dragged away,' likely referring to the transition to the afterlife or a supernatural journey.
  • 忽高忽下 - Literally 'suddenly high and low,' likely describing a disorienting or dream-like experience.
  • 如随风败箨 - A metaphor comparing the experience to a withered leaf being blown by the wind, symbolizing a loss of control or detachment.
  • 倏然惊醒 - Literally 'suddenly woke up,' marking the end of the experience and the return to reality.
  • 具道之 - Literally 'explained it in detail,' indicating that the narrator is recounting the experience to someone else.

  • 无心作过 - Refers to unintentional wrongdoing, a key concept in moral and legal philosophy.
  • 甲子七月 - Refers to a specific date, likely in the Chinese calendar, marking the time of the event.
